themes.label: Nuclear / AI Power
themes.narrative: AI will require massive clean baseload power. Nuclear — specifically SMRs — is the only viable answer. The EPA is signaling support for SMR buildout and hyperscalers are signing LOIs.
themes.dataAnchor: OKLO -26% (1m), SMR -23%, URA -22%, CEG -22%. No signed PPAs at commercial scale. NRC permitting timeline: 5-7 years minimum. The active event signal shows 6 news hits for 'Nuclear SMR Accelerated Permitting' but zero contracted revenue in our tracked universe. EPA chief commentary is political signaling, not a capital commitment.
themes.counterRead: The thesis is directionally correct — AI does need power, and nuclear is part of the long-run answer. But the market priced 2027 delivery into names where 2032 is the realistic timeline. The reset is the market repricing the timeline, not the thesis. Re-entry is warranted when permit milestones are real (first NRC construction approval) or when a major hyperscaler signs a binding PPA.
themes.watchFor: Binding PPA announcement (Microsoft/Google/Amazon + nuclear operator). First NRC construction permit issued for a light-water SMR design. CEG or OKLO revenue guidance with contracted megawatts.

themes.label: Credit / Macro
themes.narrative: The market is pricing rising recession fears. Rate hikes damaged growth, the consumer is weakening, and the equity selloff signals deteriorating confidence.
themes.dataAnchor: HY OAS: 2.75% — historically tight. Not pricing credit stress. SPY is 7.8% above its 200d MA (~$684). Yield curve: 2s10s +41bp (steepening, not inverted — historically a recovery signal). Real 10Y: 2.08%. DFF: 3.62% with cuts still underway. The data describes a soft landing in progress, not a recession.
themes.counterRead: The fear narrative is not supported by the credit market. HY spreads widen before recessions — they're tightening. The equity selloff is a multiple compression event (growth stocks repricing on duration sensitivity) not a credit event. The Kostolany cycle supports Phase D: rate cuts deepening, credit available, economy stabilizing. The posture is Hold and sharpen, not Defend and reduce.
themes.watchFor: HY OAS above 4.0% (credit starting to price stress). SPY breaks below 200d MA (~$684). Fed pauses or reverses cuts — DFF stops falling. 10Y yield spikes above 5% on inflation re-acceleration.

themes.label: Software / SaaS compounders
themes.narrative: AI is commoditizing software. GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, and open models are eating SaaS ARR. Enterprise buyers are pausing renewals. The multiple compression in software is structural, not cyclical.
themes.dataAnchor: GTLB: revenue +23.1% YoY, stock -40.7% from 52wH. HUBS: revenue +23.4% YoY, stock -65.6% from 52wH — CTO bought $2.59M open-market. NOW: revenue +22.1% YoY, stock -46% from 52wH. RDDT: +8.4% (1m) with data licensing and DAU growth intact. The data shows businesses growing 20-60% while prices have collapsed 40-65%. GTLB earnings this month will be the first real AI-attach-rate proof point.
themes.counterRead: The narrative that AI kills SaaS is not showing up in the revenue lines yet. The stocks are down because growth investors sold duration-sensitive assets as real rates rose — not because the businesses are breaking. When earnings prove AI is additive to ARR (GTLB AI Duo attach rate, HUBS AI CRM), the narrative flips and these re-rate sharply. HUBS with insider confirmation is the clearest signal the data is ahead of the narrative.
themes.watchFor: GTLB Q1 FY2027 earnings: ARR beat + AI Duo attach rate > 15%. HUBS Q2 2026: SMB ARR stabilization + AI CRM net new ARR. NOW Q2 2026 cRPO acceleration. Any major SaaS name cutting guidance on AI competitive pressure (would validate the narrative).

themes.label: Financial rails
themes.narrative: Digitization of payments is a multi-decade secular trend. Fintech is compressing margins at the edges but the core rails (Visa, MA) are structurally protected.
themes.dataAnchor: MA: +1.6% today while QQQ -3.2% and SPY -1.7%. MA: -0.4% over 1 month vs QQQ -2.5% and SPY -1.4%. This relative outperformance is a signal. Steepening yield curve (2s10s +41bp) directly benefits payment networks through improved float income. Dollar strength (+2.1% over 1m) benefits dollar-denominated global transaction volumes.
themes.counterRead: No significant divergence. The narrative and the price action agree. MA is behaving exactly as it should in a risk-compression / quality rotation environment. It is not a high-upside opportunity from here — it is the right place to be already positioned. If you're not in it, this is not a chase setup. It confirms the broader thesis: credit-quality businesses with real transaction volume hold in risk-off, and re-rate in Phase D.
themes.watchFor: MA quarterly transaction volume growth below 5% (would signal consumer slowdown). Dollar reversal weakening below DXY 97 (would reduce international transaction tailwind). Fintech regulatory action targeting interchange fees.

themes.label: AI infrastructure (hardware)
themes.narrative: AI capex is infinite. Every hyperscaler is committing $50B+ to data centers. NVDA, AVGO, VRT, and power infrastructure cannot keep up with demand.
themes.dataAnchor: AVGO: -13.1% (1m), -5.8% today. VRT: -17.7% (1m), -6.9% today. GEV: -14.6% (1m). NXT: -7.7% (1m). These names ran 12-15% in 3 months then sold off hard. AVGO Apollo/$35B deal is real (matched in event signals) but the multiple already priced perfect execution. VRT RSI: 33.6 — oversold but not yet at a dislocation depth that warrants entry (still only -26% from 52wH for a high-multiple stock). TMDX earnings miss (materiality 8) is a reminder that even thesis-correct names miss.
themes.counterRead: The AI capex thesis is real but the market over-indexed on it in Q1. The correction is healthy — it's moving from 'narrative multiple' to 'show me the order books.' VRT and NXT earnings in July will determine whether the order rate justifies the re-rating. If Q2 order books are strong, these snap back sharply. If they disappoint, the reset goes deeper. AVGO custom ASIC supercycle has an Apollo $35B backing — the institutional money is real. Wait for earnings to confirm before re-entering.
themes.watchFor: VRT Q2 2026: data center cooling order rate + margin guidance. NXT Q1 FY2027: tracker shipment guidance + tariff impact clarity. AVGO Q3 FY2026: custom ASIC pipeline size. Any hyperscaler capex guidance cut (would break the thesis).

strategyPosture: Hold and sharpen. No new positions without a thesis-confirming catalyst — earnings season (July–August) is the next real decision point. The macro environment does not support defensive action: credit is clean, the 200d MA is intact, this is a narrative reset not a structural crack.
whereWaveBuilds: Software compounders with AI-attach-rate proof (GTLB earnings this month is the first test). Financial rails (MA relative strength is already leading). Phase D compounders at deep dislocation depth — HUBS with insider confirmation at -65.6% from high is the clearest data-ahead-of-narrative setup in the current universe.
watchFor: HY OAS above 4.0% → posture shifts to defensive
SPY breaks 200d MA (~$684) → structural breakdown signal, re-evaluate all positions
GTLB Q1 FY2027 earnings: ARR + AI Duo attach rate → software thesis proof or invalidation
VRT/NXT Q2 order books → AI infrastructure thesis proof or deeper reset
Nuclear: first binding hyperscaler PPA at commercial scale → nuclear re-enters as fundamental, not narrative
